context.description: A philosophical nonfiction passage argues that a young man’s choices are shaped by mechanisms beyond free choice. It then shifts to a guided thought experiment about how perception of a woman, sexual cues, and internal bodily reactions alter motivation and priorities.
normalization_notes: Source text is German; translated visible values into English control-language.
Book title inferred from source file name.

rows.paragraph_text: The passage argues that the young man’s actions were determined by mechanisms beyond real choice, then expands the scenario into a guided thought experiment. It examines how external signs of attraction and the man’s internal physiological reactions reshape his priorities and perceived options.

content.free_text: book_page book_body_content theoretical_discussion Nonfiction philosophical discussion of free will, perception, attraction, and internal bodily response. Guided thought experiment on how external cues and internal states alter motivation and choice.
